full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Designing the kernel degree ablation experiment
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: For Task 3.1 Ablation 1: design an experiment varying arc-cosine kernel degree n=0,1,2 at fixed depth l=1. Provide markdown explaining the component's role and Python code to run the ablation and plot results as a bar chart saved to partB/results/ablation_1.png.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Structured the ablation with a clear component description linking degree to activation functions (step/ramp/quarter-pipe) and provided the experimental loop and bar chart code.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Changed the bar colors from default matplotlib to custom hex colors for better visual distinction. Wrote the 7-sentence interpretation paragraph myself based on the actual experimental results.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified that the three degrees correspond to the correct activation functions in Figure 1 of the paper. Confirmed the experiment isolates degree from depth by fixing l=1.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Designing the multilayer depth ablation experiment
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: For Task 3.1 Ablation 2: design an experiment comparing depth l=1,2,3,4 with n=1 kernel at all layers. Explain the role of multilayer composition (Eqs 12-13) and provide code for the experiment and visualization saved to partB/results/ablation_2.png.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Produced the depth comparison loop and a color-coded bar chart distinguishing ablated (single-layer) from full (multilayer) configurations.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Added the matplotlib Patch legend to clearly mark single-layer as 'ablated' vs multilayer as 'full'. Wrote the 7-sentence interpretation paragraph myself, connecting the diminishing returns observation to the paper's Figures 2-3.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified that using n=1 at all layers follows the paper's empirical recommendation in Section 2.4. Confirmed the recursive kernel computation matches Eqs 12-13.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Writing the interpretation paragraph for degree ablation
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Help me draft a 5-7 sentence interpretation for the degree ablation results. The n=1 kernel performed best. Connect this to the ReLU dominance in deep learning, the magnitude-discarding property of n=0, and the dynamic range amplification of n=2.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Provided a draft interpretation connecting results to the paper's properties (Section 2.1) and broader machine learning context.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified the magnitude properties: k_0(x,x)=1, k_1(x,x)=||x||^2, k_2(x,x)~||x||^4 against the paper's Section 2.1.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Design an ablation varying kernel degree n=0,1,2 at fixed depth with component description and bar chart code.
top_5_prompts.why_important: Defined the first ablation experiment structure — the core deliverable of Task 3.1 Component 1.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Design an ablation comparing depth l=1,2,3,4 with n=1 kernel, explaining multilayer composition's role.
top_5_prompts.why_important: Defined the second ablation experiment — directly testing the paper's claim about depth benefits.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Draft a 5-7 sentence interpretation for the degree ablation connecting to ReLU dominance and magnitude properties.
top_5_prompts.why_important: Helped articulate the analytical interpretation connecting results to paper-specific theory.

student_declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for Task 3.1 of Part B.
